images.caption:
Figure 1:The inverted deployment ladder for single-stream 7–8B decode.
Left: achieved fraction of peak HBM bandwidth at Qwen-2.5-7B ctx=2048{=}2048batch 1 across four NVIDIA GPUs; L4 reaches 79% of its peak, H100 only
27%. Right: step time for the same workload after applying the best
lever per GPU; L4 with GPTQ+{+}ExLlamaV2 (17.36 ms) closes most of the
gap to H100 with CUDA Graphs (11.78 ms) despite the H100 having11×11\timesthe peak HBM bandwidth.

Figure 5:CUDA Graphs A/B speedup versus context length and batch size on
H100 and L4. The H100 b=1{=}1ctx=2048{=}2048point is theN=10N{=}10mean
(Table2); shading is the 95% bootstrap CI on the
mean. Other H100 b=1{=}1points are single-session from the v11 main
sweep. H100 b=4{=}4is the v13 batch sweep
(Table4). L4 b=1{=}1replicates to four significant
figures across the v12 sweep. L4 b=4{=}4is omitted: every cell OOMs.
The dotted line at1.15×1.15\timesis the pre-registered falsification
threshold.

Figure 6:Eager and graphed H100 ctx=2048{=}2048b=1{=}1step times underN=10N{=}10(this work) andN=3N{=}3(v12). TheN=3N{=}3eager outlier at
27.28 ms drives the 35.3% CV reported in earlier versions; theN=10N{=}10eager distribution is tight (CV 0.9%) and bounds the noise
floor decisively. The graphed distribution is tight in both samples.

header: 6Software stack matrix: SDPA backends, FlashAttention, FlashInfer

images.caption:
Figure 7:Per-layer attentionp50p_{50}on H100 with explicit SDPA backend
selection plus FlashAttention-3 and FlashInfer. Default SDPA (top,
highlighted) is faster than every pinned backend includingFLASH_ATTENTION, FlashInfer and FA-3 at this single-decode
shape (Llama-3-8B, n_q_heads=32{=}32, n_kv_heads=8{=}8,
head_dim=128{=}128, ctx=2048{=}2048, bf16, H100). Error bars are SD across
three sessions.CUDNN_ATTENTIONis not supported for this
shape on H100 SXM5 PyTorch 2.8+cu126. The kernel choice is not the
binding constraint; the launch sequence is.

Figure 8:L4 quantisation step times for Qwen-2.5-7B at ctx=2048{=}2048. bf16
(62.32 ms), bnb-nf4 (59.36 ms), AutoAWQ+{+}Marlin (45.24 ms),
GPTQ+{+}ExLlamaV2 EXL2 4.25bpw (17.36 ms). The Marlin kernel was tuned
for SM80 (Ampere); ExLlamaV2’s int4 GEMMs are tuned for SM89 (Ada) and
recover most of the bandwidth saving the bit-width reduction predicts.

Figure 9:Cost-per-million-tokens-served at batch-1 streaming decode,
Qwen-2.5-7B ctx=2048{=}2048, derived from per-step time and Modal published
hourly rates as of May 2026. The L4 with ExLlamaV2 serves the same
workload at substantially lower $/Mtok than the H100 with CUDA Graphs.
Numbers depend on hourly rates and exclude idle, networking and storage;
the ladder direction is the point.
